description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: city in Stanislaus County, California, United States
description_wiki: Turlock is a city in Stanislaus County, California, United States.

description: Internment camp for Japanese-Americans during World War II
description_wiki: The Gila River War Relocation Center was an American concentration camp in Arizona, one of several built by the War Relocation Authority (WRA) during the Second World War for the incarceration of Japanese Americans from the West Coast of the United States.

description: island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Hawaii (  hə-WY-ee; Hawaiian: Hawaiʻi [həˈvɐjʔi, həˈwɐjʔi]) is an island state of the United States, in the Pacific Ocean about 2,000 miles (3,200 km) southwest of the U.S. mainland.

description: consolidated city and county in California, United States
description_wiki: San Francisco, officially the City and County of San Francisco, is the fourth-most populous city in California and the 17th-most populous in the United States, with a population of 826,079 in 2025.

surface_forms: took over their place in Lafayette
description: city in Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Lafayette (formerly La Fayette) is a city in Contra Costa County, California, United States.

description: city in Contra Costa County, California, United States
description_wiki: Concord ( KON-kerd) is the most populous city in Contra Costa County, California, United States.

description: city and county seat of Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Oakland is a city in the East Bay region of the San Francisco Bay Area in the U.S. state of California.

description: city in Maricopa County, Arizona, United States
description_wiki: Litchfield Park is a city in Maricopa County, Arizona, United States.

description: city in Maricopa County, Arizona, United States
description_wiki: Glendale () is a city in Maricopa County, Arizona, United States.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: organized incorporated territory of the United States, 1898–1959
description_wiki: The Territory of Hawaii or Hawaii Territory (Hawaiian: Panalāʻau o Hawaiʻi) was an organized incorporated territory of the United States that existed from April 30, 1900, until August 21, 1959, when most of its territory was admitted to the United States as the 50th US state, the State of Hawaii.

description_wiki: Happy Valley, California may refer to:

description: city in Alameda County, California, United States
description_wiki: Berkeley (  BURK-lee) is a city on the eastern shore of San Francisco Bay in northern Alameda County, California, United States.

description: Catholic high school in Bloomington, Illinois, United States

surface_forms: all Japanese American combat unit, the 442nd
description: infantry regiment of the United States Army
description_wiki: The 442nd Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Army.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

aliases: Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States
Veterans of Foreign Wars of the U.S.
description: organization of U.S. war veterans
description_wiki: The Veterans of Foreign Wars (VFW), formally the Veterans of Foreign Wars of the United States, is an organization of United States war veterans who fought in wars, campaigns, and expeditions on foreign land, waters, or airspace as military service members.

description: World War II internment camp for Japanese-Americans
description_wiki: The Rohwer War Relocation Center was a World War II Japanese American concentration camp located in rural southeastern Arkansas, in Desha County.

description: national monument in California
description_wiki: The Tule Lake War Relocation Center, also known as the Tule Lake Segregation Center, was an American concentration camp located in Modoc and Siskiyou counties in California and constructed in 1942 by the United States government to incarcerate Japanese Americans, forcibly removing them from their homes on the West Coast of the United States.

description: Term for Japanese Americans
description_wiki: Kibei (kibei (帰米; literally "go home to America")) refers to Japanese Americans born in the United States whose parents had sent them to receive their education in Japan and who had then returned to the US by the time that the US and Japan became engaged in war in the 1940s.

aliases: World War II Japanese American Internment Museum
description: history museum in McGehee, Arkansas
